title: Accelerating the prediction of stable materials with machine learning
methods: crystal graph convolutional neural networks (CGCNN, iCGCNN, MEGNet, AMDNet)
transfer learning and multi-task learning (ElemNet, AtomSets, MODNet)
ML interatomic potentials (MTP, SNAP, GAP, Behler-Parrinello NNPs)
data_sources: Materials Project (DFT formation enthalpies and crystal structures)
JARVIS (Joint Automated Repository for Various Integrated Simulations)
Experimental HEA dataset (401 high-entropy alloy compositions)
Hydrocarbon molecular crystal dataset (444 crystals for vibrational ML)

data_scope.note: Review article; no new dataset produced. Scope drawn from cited studies.
data_scope.oqmd_training_subset: 96,000 QH + 184,000 TH + 146,000 NH compounds (Kim et al.)
data_scope.temperature_range: 0 K to 1800 K (SISSO Gibbs energy study)

core_conclusion: Machine learning is now a rapid and powerful alternative to DFT for predicting materials thermodynamic stability. Crystal graph CNNs, ML interatomic potentials, and active-learning campaigns have confirmed hundreds to thousands of new stable compounds at T=0 and at finite temperature. The frontier is extending ML to vibrational free energy, configurational disorder, pressure, and surface energy where training data are scarce.
why_it_matters: Breaks the combinatorial barrier of billions of possible compounds; bridges zero- and finite-temperature stability prediction; demonstrates closed-loop autonomous materials discovery; charts an honest roadmap of remaining ML gaps (pressure, surface energy, synthesizability).
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary synthesis of ML and condensed-matter physics; forward-looking Outlook section defines community targets; broad societal relevance to energy and manufacturing; highlights open-database infrastructure (Materials Project, OQMD, AFLOW, JARVIS, NOMAD, OPTIMADE).
added_value_to_my_research: Held-out-zone evaluation protocol from materials ML -> spatial cross-validation benchmark for GeoAI models across cities
Uncertainty-driven active learning for interatomic potentials -> GP-uncertainty acquisition for spatially sparse labeled health/demand data
Contrastive pre-training on unlabeled crystal graphs -> contrastive pre-training on satellite/street-view tiles for geospatial foundation models
ML stability under environmental stress (MOFs) -> facility network resilience under population/demand perturbations in urban health
Physics-infused heterogeneous GNN with thermodynamic state nodes -> city-level heterogeneous GNN with spatial autocorrelation priors for cross-city health accessibility transfer

related.title: A framework to evaluate machine learning crystal stability predictions
related.relation: standardized benchmark for evaluating the ML stability models reviewed in this paper

related.title: Uncertainty-driven dynamics for active learning of interatomic potentials
related.relation: active learning protocol directly addressing the small-training-set bottleneck for ML potentials reviewed here

related.title: Molecular contrastive learning of representations via graph neural networks
related.relation: contrastive pre-training on molecular graphs -- extension path for crystal graph encoders in small vibrational-data regime

related.title: Prediction of water stability of metal-organic frameworks using machine learning
related.relation: applies the same convex-hull ML stability framework to MOFs, extending this review's scope to hybrid materials

related.title: Deep learning accelerates discovery of complex nanomaterials
related.relation: physics-infused heterogeneous GNN unifying multiple material property predictions -- the architectural synthesis the review calls for

figures.caption: Convex hull varying with temperature: stable phases at T=0 differ from those at finite T
